title: Three generations of drought; only one source still gives
Marn the well-tender keeps the cistern at the Cistern Steps
Marn's pilgrimage of dread; the spring runs clean and he cannot say why
A traveller with a wend-pipe presses the low seam where wall meets rock
The traveller finds a worn keeper's mark below the wet line
The Loom Quarter gone quiet; Pell works the last loom
An old quarrel of the craft-row, forgotten; Pell knows nothing of it
Ode the field-warden keeps the only crossing of the Wend Fields
Ode digs up a boundary-stone set where the ford holds firm
The folk cross by the stone; none know its origin
A living bridge-tree spans the dry gorge
Tamsin the far-field settler crosses the bridge-tree to the far ground
The bearer plays the wend-pipe into the Founding Age and sees Sera seal the vent and Halden mark the ford
The hollow withers on; the water, the ford, and the bridge-tree hold
